dialogos.texto_en: Hmm...
What's wrong? You look troubled.
Ah, Uesugi-kun!\nPerfect timing.
It's about the name of this park.\nWhat is 'Kaiyohaku' referring to?
What, that's what was troubling you?
I was looking at the map trying to decide where to go,\nand I got curious.
It's referring to the Okinawa International\nOcean Expo. This park was built on the site\nof the exposition, so they named it 'Kaiyohaku.'
Expo...\nAh, exposition, exposition, right.
(Her reaction seems like Yotsuba...)
...Do you not know what an exposition is?
No, I just forgot for a moment.
You know, like the 19th-century\nParis Exposition they teach in world history?
Oh, yes! I remember that.
I think, that was like an exhibition\nwhere various countries\nbring various things to showcase?
...Yeah, pretty much.\nSo 'exposition' is another name to call the 'world's fair.'\nIn other words%p-2;――
Uh, Uesugi-kun?\nLet's not talk about studying or anything\nat times like this.
After all, this is our graduation trip.\nLet's enjoy ourselves, and forget about studying!
Your university classes are starting soon,\nare you okay with that?
Don't you worry about that.\nI'll work hard to achieve my dream.
(...Is this Itsuki herself?\n Yotsuba has also decided to go to university,\n though...)
...Hm? What's up?
Uesugi-kun, do you think you'll be able \nto give a proper answer?
O-of course!
...You've said you've made up your mind,\nand I believe in those words.
Yeah...
Please, do your best...\nIs it perhaps weird to say that?
The deadline is tomorrow evening.
I'll give you a proper answer.\nI'll definitely see through your disguises,\nand win this game!
Hey, somethin'll work out.
That's not a very reliable response.\nAre you seriously considering it?
Of course, but that's all I can say for now.\nI'm not trying to show off.
I see.\nWell, I'm relieved\nthat you're at least somewhat positive.
The deadline is tomorrow evening.
I'll give you a proper answer.\nI'll definitely see through your disguises,\nand win this game!
I don't want to disappoint your expectations.
That isn't an answer, you know?
...You should understand what I mean.
Teehee, that's just like you.
I understand.\nEven if it's not an answer, it's enough.
The deadline is tomorrow evening.
I'll give you a proper answer.\nI'll definitely see through your disguises,\nand win this game!
Are things, really okay?
